twistCard: Demo Day is in 36 hours. Every founder you know is in their hotel room rehearsing the same number with a different straight face.

scenes.title: Scene 9 · The Reckoning
scenes.imagePrompt: the empty co-working space at 2am, fluorescent strip lights flickering, female cofounder packing a single cardboard box on the desk, the founder watching from a doorway, wide two-shot, exhausted resignation

dialogue.speaker: cofounder
dialogue.text: I'm leaving. I emailed the board fifteen minutes ago.

dialogue.speaker: cofounder
dialogue.text: You can keep my equity vesting if you want. I'd rather you bought it back at the cap. Either way.

dialogue.speaker: cofounder
dialogue.text: One last thing. The pitch on Thursday — the MRR slide. Don't say ten million. I'll know. Everyone in that room will know.

choices.label: Beg her to stay
choices.consequence: Founder pleads; the cofounder gives a 30-day ultimatum.

choices.label: Buy back her equity
choices.consequence: Founder cleans the cap table; the cofounder leaves quietly.

choices.label: Let her walk
choices.consequence: Founder accepts it; the door clicks shut at 2:14am.

scenes.title: Scene 10 · The Backstage Pep Talk
scenes.imagePrompt: backstage at a Demo Day venue, the YC partner with reading glasses and a clipboard, founder in a hoodie clutching a water bottle, exposed pipework and stage-light glow above, intimate two-shot, pre-stage tension

dialogue.speaker: mentor
dialogue.text: You're on in ten minutes. Five hundred people in that room, half of them writing checks.

dialogue.speaker: mentor
dialogue.text: The number they remember is the one you say on stage. It lives forever.

dialogue.speaker: mentor
dialogue.text: Your actual MRR is eighty thousand. That's real. That's respectable. But I've seen founders say ten million ARR with a straight face and walk out with a term sheet. Your call.

choices.label: Understated truth
choices.consequence: Founder commits to the real number; the room quiets but doesn't reach for checkbooks.

choices.label: Full hype. Ten mil ARR.
choices.consequence: Founder lies on stage; three texts from VCs hit before the next demo finishes.

choices.label: Round generously
choices.consequence: Founder fudges to 'mid-seven figures'; nobody fact-checks live.

scenes.title: Scene 11 · The Hater Comes Back
scenes.imagePrompt: the after-party at Trick Dog in the Mission, mezcal cocktails on a small table, the competing-startup CEO blocking the founder's exit, neon and warm bar light, tense two-shot, late-night closing time

dialogue.speaker: hater
dialogue.text: Heard your pitch. Bold numbers. Aspirational, even.

dialogue.speaker: hater
dialogue.text: I have receipts. Stripe export. Three months of MRR. I haven't tweeted it yet. I might not.

dialogue.speaker: hater
dialogue.text: What's it worth to you? Acquihire, advisory role, board observer — make me a number.

choices.label: Call his bluff
choices.consequence: Founder dares him; the receipts are real and they hit Twitter at 11:42pm.

choices.label: Cut him in
choices.consequence: Founder offers an advisory slot; the receipts disappear into a NDA.

choices.label: Walk past him
choices.consequence: Founder ignores the threat; he tweets within the hour either way.

scenes.title: Scene 12 · The Last Office Hours
scenes.imagePrompt: a quiet Palo Alto cafe at 8am, the YC partner pouring two cups of coffee from a French press, founder seated across from her with a closed laptop, soft morning light, intimate two-shot, the calm after

dialogue.speaker: mentor
dialogue.text: You made it through the week. That puts you ahead of about thirty percent of your batch already.

dialogue.speaker: mentor
dialogue.text: So. The real question. When the Bloomberg piece runs in eighteen months, what's the headline you want?

dialogue.speaker: mentor
dialogue.text: Don't tell me. Tell yourself. And then ship something this week that proves it.

choices.label: Build quietly. No press.
choices.consequence: Founder commits to silence; the next news about Wagr is a Series B.

choices.label: Lean in. Be the story.
choices.consequence: Founder embraces the persona; the podcast tour books out the month.

choices.label: I don't know.
choices.consequence: Founder admits uncertainty; the mentor smiles and orders another coffee.
